svg.text: — a teleprompter you can actually use
Two displays: LEFT = normal screen · RIGHT = teleprompter (beam-splitter glass + camera)
How the mirroring and the mouse behave, before and after.
the one display ScreenFlip flips — everything else is untouched
How: ScreenFlip captures a headless virtual workspace and re-renders it horizontally flipped with ScreenCaptureKit.
The cursor lives natively on that workspace, so clicks, drags and typing just work; a proxy cursor is drawn at the mirrored spot.
No mouse or keyboard input is ever intercepted, and there is no low-level framebuffer flip.
Correct reflection OR a usable cursor — never both
